aitareya.sk:
ॐ आत्मा वा इदमेक एवाग्र आसीन्नान्यत्किंचन मिषत् ।  
स ईक्षत लोकान्नु सृजा इति ॥ १ ॥
aitareya.en:
In the beginning the Spirit was One and all  
this (universe) was the Spirit; there was  
nought else that saw. The Spirit thought,  
“Lo, I will make me worlds from out my being.”

aitareya.sk:
स इमाँ ल्लोकानसृजत ।  
अम्भो मरीचीर्मापोऽदोऽम्भः परेण दिवं  
द्यौः प्रतिष्ठाऽन्तरिक्षं मरीचयः ॥  
पृथिवी मरो या अधस्तात्त आपः ॥ २ ॥
aitareya.en:
He created these worlds:  
Ambhah, the world of water—bearing clouds,  
Marichi, the world of the solar rays,  
Mara, the world of mortals and Ap, the world of waters.  
Yon is Ambhah, above heaven; heaven is its support.  
The Marichis are the interspace. Mara is the earth. What is underneath is Ap.

aitareya.sk:
स ईक्षतेमे नु लोका लोकपालान्नु सृजा इति ॥  
सोऽद्भ्य एव पुरुषं समुद्धृत्यामूर्छयत् ॥ ३ ॥
aitareya.en:
He bethought Himself: "Here now are the worlds.  
Let Me now create world—guardians."  
Right from the waters He drew forth the  
Person in the form of a lump and gave Him a shape.

aitareya.sk:
तमभ्यतपत्तस्याभितप्तस्य मुखं निरभिद्यत यथाऽण्डं  
मुखाद्वाग्वाचोऽग्निर्नासिके निरभिद्येतं नासिकाभ्यां प्राणः ॥
प्राणाद्वायुरक्षिणी निरभिद्येतमक्षीभ्यां चक्षुश्चक्षुष  
आदित्यः कर्णौ निरभिद्येतां कर्णाभ्यां श्रोत्रं  
श्रोत्रद्दिशस्त्वङ्निरभिद्यत त्वचो लोमानि लोमभ्य ओषधिवनस्पतयो  
हृदयं निरभिद्यत हृदयान्मनो मनसश्चन्द्रमा नाभिर्निरभिद्यत  
नाभ्या अपानोऽपानान्मृत्युः  
शिश्नं निरभिद्यत शिश्नाद्रेतो रेतस आपः ॥ ४ ॥

ता एता देवताः सृष्टा अस्मिन्महत्यर्णवे प्रापतन् ।  
तमशनापिपासाभ्यामन्ववार्जत् ।  
ता एनमब्रुवन्नायतनं नः प्रजानीहि यस्मिन्प्रतिष्ठिता अन्नमदामेति ॥ १ ॥
aitareya.en:
These deities, thus created, fell into this great ocean.  
He subjected the Person to hunger and thirst.  
They said to Him: "Find out for us an abode  
wherein being established we may eat food."

aitareya.sk:
अग्निर्वाग्भूत्वा मुखं प्राविशद्वायुः प्राणो भूत्वा नासिके  
प्राविशदादित्यश्चक्षुर्भूत्वाऽक्षिणी प्राविशाद्दिशः  
श्रोत्रं भूत्वा कर्णौ प्राविशन्नोषधिवनस्पतयो लोमानि भूत्वा  
त्वचंप्राविशंश्चन्द्रमा मनो भूत्वा हृदयं प्राविशन्मृत्युरपानो  
भूत्वा नाभिं प्राविशदापो रेतो भूत्वा शिश्नं प्राविशन् ॥ ४ ॥
aitareya.en:
The deity fire became the organ of speech and entered the mouth.  
Air became breath and entered the nostrils.  
The sun became sight and entered the eyes;  
the quarters of space became hearing and entered the ears.  
Plants and trees, the deity of air, became hairs and entered the skin.  
The moon became the mind and entered the heart.  
Death became the apana and entered the navel.  
The waters became semen and entered the virile member.

aitareya.sk:
तमशनायापिपासे अब्रूतामावाभ्यामभिप्रजानीहीति ते अब्रवीदेतास्वेव  
वां देवतास्वाभजाम्येतासु भागिन्न्यौ करोमीति ।  
तस्माद्यस्यै कस्यै च देवतायै हविर्गृह्यते  
भागिन्यावेवास्यामशनायापिपासे भवतः ॥ ५ ॥
aitareya.en:
Hunger and thirst said to the Creator: "For the two of us find an abode also."  
He said to them: "I assign the two of you to these deities;  
I make you co—sharers with them." Therefore to whatsoever deity an oblation is made,  
hunger and thirst became sharers in it.

## Chapter III — The Embodiment of the Supreme Self

aitareya.sk: स ईक्षतेमे नु लोकाश्च लोकपालाश्चान्नमेभ्यः सृजा इति ॥ १ ॥
aitareya.en:
He bethought Himself: "Here now are the worlds and the world—guardians.  
Let Me cerate food for them."

aitareya.sk:
सोऽपोऽभ्यतपत्ताभ्योऽभितप्ताभ्यो मूर्तिरजायत ।  
या वै सा मूर्तिरजायतान्नं वै तत् ॥ २ ॥
aitareya.en:
He brooded over the waters. From the waters,  
thus brooded over, there emerged a condensed form.  
The form that so emerged is indeed food.

aitareya.sk:
तदेनत्सृष्टं पराङ्त्यजिघांसत्तद्वाचाऽजिघृक्षत् तन्नाशक्नोद्वाचा ग्रहीतुम् ।  
स यद्धैनद्वाचाऽग्रहैष्यदभिव्याहृत्य हैवान्नमत्रप्स्यत् ॥ ३ ॥
aitareya.en:
The food so created wished to flee away.  
He sought to grasp it with speech.  
But He was not able to grasp it with speech.  
If, indeed, He has grasped it with speech,  
one would then have been satisfied by merely uttering the word food

aitareya.sk:
स ईक्षत कथं न्विदं मदृते स्यादिति स ईक्षत कतरेण प्रपद्या इति ।  
स ईक्षत यदि वाचाऽभिव्याहृतं यदि प्राणेनाभिप्राणितं यदि चक्षुषा  
दृष्टं यदि श्रोत्रेण श्रुतं यदि त्वचा स्पृष्टं यदि मनसा ध्यातं यद्यपानेनाभ्यपानितं  
यदि शिश्नेन विसृष्टमथ कोऽहमिति ॥ ११ ॥
aitareya.en:
He bethought Himself: "How could this exist without Me?"  
Then He said to Himself: "Which way shall I enter it?"  
he said to Himself further: "If speech is uttered by the organ of speech,  
if smelling is done by the breath, seeing by the eyes,  
hearing by the ears, touching by the skin,  
thinking by the mind, eating by the apana and  
the emission of semen by the virile member, them who am I?"

aitareya.sk:
स एतमेव सीमानं विदर्यैतया द्वारा प्रापद्यत ।  
सैषा विदृतिर्नाम द्वास्तदेतन्नाऽन्दनम् ।  
तस्य त्रय आवसथास्त्रयः स्वप्ना  
अयमावसथोऽयमावसथोऽयमावसथ इति ॥ १२ ॥
aitareya.en:
So, piercing the end, the Lord entered through that door.  
That door is known as the vidriti, the cleft. This is the place of bliss.  
Atman, thus embodied, has three abodes, three conditions of sleep.  
This is one abode, this is another, this is the third.

aitareya.sk:
स जातो भूतान्यभिव्यैख्यत् किमिहान्यं वावदिषदिति ।  
स एतमेव पुरुषं ब्रह्म ततममपश्यत् ।  
इदमदर्शनमिती ३ ॥ १३ ॥
aitareya.en:
Having been born as the jiva, He realised the elements as one with Himself.  
What else here would one desire to speak about?  
He perceived this very person as the all—pervading Brahman.  
He said: "Ah, I have seen It."

aitareya.sk:
तस्मादिदन्द्रो नामेदन्द्रो ह वै नाम ।  
तमिदन्द्रं सन्तमिंद्र इत्याचक्षते परोक्षेण ।  
परोक्षप्रिया इव हि देवाः परोक्षप्रिया इव हि देवाः ॥ १४ ॥
aitareya.en:
Therefore He is called Idandra. Idandra, indeed is His name.  
Him who is Idandra they call indirectly Indra.  
For the gods appear to be fond of cryptic epithets;  
yea, the gods appear to be fond of cryptic epithets.

# Part Two

## Chapter I — The Three Births of the Self

aitareya.sk:
ॐ पुरुषे ह वा अयमादितो गर्भो भवति यदेतद्रेतः ।  
तदेतत्सर्वेभ्योऽङ्गेभ्यस्तेजः संभूतमात्मन्येवऽऽत्मानं बिभर्ति  
तद्यदा स्त्रियां सिञ्चत्यथैनज्जनयति तदस्य प्रथमं जन्म ॥ १ ॥
aitareya.en:
This person is, at first, the germ in a man.  
That which is the semen is here called the germ.  
This semen is the vigour drawn from all the limbs.  
The man bears the self in the self.  
When he pours the semen into a woman, he gives it a birth.  
This, indeed, is the first birth of the embodied soul.

aitareya.sk:
तत्स्त्रिया आत्मभूयं गच्छति यथा स्वमङ्गं तथा ।  
तस्मादेनां न हिनस्ति ।  
साऽस्यैतमात्मानमत्र गतं भावयति ॥ २ ॥
aitareya.en:
That semen becomes one with the woman — just like a limb of her own.  
That is why it does not hurt her.  
She nourishes this self of his that has come into her.

aitareya.sk:
सा भावयित्री भावयितव्या भवति । तं स्त्री गर्भ बिभर्ति ।  
सोऽग्र एव कुमारं जन्मनोऽग्रेऽधिभावयति ।  
स यत्कुमारं जन्मनोऽग्रेऽधिभावयत्यात्मानमेव तद्भावयत्येषं लोकानां सन्तत्या ।  
एवं सन्तता हीमे लोकास्तदस्य द्वितीयं जन्म ॥ ३ ॥
aitareya.en:
She, being the nourisher, should be nourished.  
The woman nourishes the embryo.  
Immediately after its birth he nourishes the child,  
which in the beginning was already born.  
Nourishing the child from birth onward,  
he thus nourishes himself for the continuation of these worlds.  
For thus alone are these worlds perpetuated. This is one's second birth.

aitareya.sk:
सोऽस्यायमात्मा पुण्येभ्यः कर्मभ्यः प्रतिधीयते ।  
अथास्यायामितर आत्मा कृतकृत्यो वयोगतः प्रैति ।  
स इतः प्रयन्नेव पुनर्जायते तदस्य तृतीयं जन्म ॥ ४ ॥
aitareya.en:
He who is the one self of his, is made his substitute for virtuous deeds.  
Then the other self of his, having accomplished his duties and  
reached his age departs. So departing hence, he is born again.  
This is the third birth.

aitareya.sk:
तदुक्तमृषिणा गर्भे नु सन्नन्वेषामवेदमहं देवानां जनिमानि  
विश्वा शतं मा पुर आयसीररक्षन्नधः श्येनो जवसा निरदीयमिति ।  
गर्भ एवैतच्छयानो वामदेव एवमुवाच ॥ ५ ॥
aitareya.en:
About this a rishi has said: "While still lying in the womb,  
I came to know all the births of the gods.  
A hundred strongholds, as if made of iron, confined me,  
yet I burst through them all swiftly, like a hawk."  
Vamadeva spoke, in this wise, even while lying in the womb.

aitareya.sk:
स एवं विद्वानस्माच्छरीरभेदादूर्ध्व उत्क्रम्यामुष्मिन् स्वर्गे लोके  
सर्वान् कामानाप्त्वाऽमृतः समभवत् समभवत् ॥ ६ ॥
aitareya.en:
Thus endowed with Knowledge, he,  
becoming one with the Supreme Self and  
soaring aloft on the dissolution of the body,  
obtained all desires in the heavenly world and  
became immortal—yea, became immortal.

# Part Three

## Chapter I — Concerning the Self

aitareya.sk:
ॐ कोऽयमात्मेति वयमुपास्महे कतरः स आत्मा ।  
येन वा पश्यति येन वा श‍ृणोति येन वा गंधानाजिघ्रति  
येन वा वाचं व्याकरोति येन वा स्वादु चास्वादु च विजानाति ॥ १ ॥
aitareya.en:
Who is He whom we worship, thinking: "This is the Self"?  
Which one is the Self? Is it He by whom one sees form,  
by whom one hears sound and by whom one tastes the sweet and the unsweet?

aitareya.sk:
यदेतद्धृदयं मनश्चैतत् । संज्ञानमाज्ञानं विज्ञानं प्रज्ञानं मेधा  
दृष्टिर्धृतिमतिर्मनीषा जूतिः स्मृतिः संकल्पः क्रतुरसुः कामो वश इति ।  
सर्वाण्येवैतानि प्रज्ञानस्य नामधेयानि भवंति ॥ २ ॥
aitareya.en:
Is it the heart and the mind. It is consciousness, lordship, knowledge,  
wisdom, retentive power of mind, sense knowledge, steadfastness, though,  
thoughtfulness, sorrow, memory, concepts, purpose, life, desire, longing:  
all these are but various names of Consciousness (Prajnanam).

aitareya.sk:
एष ब्रह्मैष इन्द्र एष प्रजापतिरेते सर्वे देवा इमानि च  
पञ्चमहाभूतानि पृथिवी वायुराकाश आपो  
ज्योतींषीत्येतानीमानि च क्षुद्रमिश्राणीव ।  
बीजानीतराणि चेतराणि चाण्डजानि च जारुजानि च स्वेदजानि चोद्भिज्जानि  
चाश्वा गावः पुरुषा हस्तिनो यत्किञ्चेदं प्राणि जङ्गमं च पतत्रि  
च यच्च स्थावरं सर्वं तत्प्रज्ञानेत्रं प्रज्ञाने प्रतिष्ठितं  
प्रज्ञानेत्रो लोकः प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठा प्रज्ञानं ब्रह्म ॥ ३ ॥

स एतेन प्राज्ञेनाऽऽत्मनाऽस्माल्लोकादुत्क्रम्यामुष्मिन्स्वर्गे लोके सर्वान्  
कामानाप्त्वाऽमृतः समभवत् समभवत् ॥ ४ ॥
aitareya.en:
He, having realised oneness with Pure Consciousness,  
soared from this world and having obtained all desires in yonder heavenly world,  
became immortal—yea, became immortal.

ekakshara.sk:
एकाक्षरं त्वक्षरेऽत्रास्ति सोमे सुषुम्नायां चेह दृढी स एकः ।  
त्वं विश्वभूर्भूतपतिः पुराणः पर्जन्य एको भुवनस्य गोप्ता ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou art the one Imperishable in the Imperishable, conjoint with Uma. As known by means of Susumna, here (on the empirical plane), the one firm (Principle art Thou). Thou art the ancient source of the world, the Lord of beings; Thou the Parjanya (the Principle of life-giving water), the Protector of the world.

ekakshara.sk:
विश्वे निमग्नपदवीः कवीनां त्वं जातवेदो भुवनस्य नाथः ।  
अजातमग्ने स हिरण्यरेता यज्ञस्त्वमेवैकविभुः पुराणः ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou art immanent in all; from (the view-point) of the wise, Thou, the Lord of the world art the all-knowing (Fire). In the beginning Thou art unborn, the first born, the sacrifice, too, art Thou. Thou alone the one omnipresent and ancient.

ekakshara.sk:
प्राणः प्रसूतिर्भुवनस्य योनिर्व्याप्तं त्वया एकपदेन विश्वम् ।  
त्वं विश्वभूर्योनिपारः स्वगर्भे कुमार एको विशिखः सुधन्वा ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou art the Principle of life; Thou the manifestation (the manifested world); Thou the source of the world; by a quarter hast Thou pervaded this world. Thou art the world’s birth, the cause, the life supreme, and the child in the womb armed with the excellent bow and arrow.

ekakshara.sk:
वितत्य बाणं तरुणार्कवर्णं व्योमान्तरे भासि हिरण्यगर्भः ।  
भासा त्वया व्योग्नि कृतः सुतार्क्ष्यस्त्वं वै कुमारस्त्वमरिष्टनेमिः ।।
ekakshara.en: Having widely spread out the arrow, shining like the youthful sun in the sky, Thou shinest as Hiranyagarbha. By means of light hast Thou created the resplendent eagle (the sun), in the sky. Thou art Subrahmanya, Thou Arishtanemi (a brother of Garuda).

ekakshara.sk:
त्वं वज्रभृद्भूतपतिस्त्वमेव कामः प्रजानां निहितोऽसि सोमे ।  
स्वाहा स्वधा यच्च वषट् करोति रुद्रः पशूनां गुहया निमग्नः ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou art the bearer of the Thunderbolt (Indra), the Lord of beings; Thou alone art love in all living beings and in Soma (Shiva with Uma). Thou art the sacred syllables Svaha, Svadha and Vasat; Thou the Rudra immanent unhurt in the hearts of beasts.

ekakshara.sk:
धाता विधाता पवनः सुपर्णो विष्णुर्वराहो रजनी रहश्च ।  
भूतं भविष्यत्प्रभवः क्रियाश्च कालः क्रमस्त्वं परमाक्षरं च ॥
ekakshara.en: (Thou art) the supporter, the ordainer, the purifier (air), the all pervading plenum, the divine Varaha, night and aloneness; the past, the future and the present; Thou art all activities, time, sequence and the supreme Imperishable.

ekakshara.sk:
ऋचो यजूंषि प्रसवन्ति वक्त्रात्सामानि सम्राड्वसुरन्तरिक्षम् ।  
त्वं यज्ञनेता हुतभुग्विभुश्च रुद्रास्तथा दैत्यगणा वसुश्च ॥
ekakshara.en: The Vedic verses, prose formulas and songs proceed from Thy mouth. Thou shinest best (as) the Vasus and the sky. Thou art the leader of the sacrifice, the all-pervading fire and the Rudras. So also (art Thou) the hosts of demons and Vasus.

ekakshara.sk:
स एष देवोऽम्बरगश्च चक्रे अन्येऽभ्यधिष्ठेत तमो निरुन्ध्यः ।  
हिरण्मयं यस्य विभाति सर्वं व्योमान्तरे रश्मिमिमंसुनाभिः ।।
ekakshara.en: This God here dwells in the sun (the wheel of the celestial chariot) and elsewhere dispelling darkness. All that pertains to Him shines in lovely (fashion) like gold in some other sky. (This world of relativities shines so).

ekakshara.sk:
स सर्ववेत्ता भुवनस्य गोप्ता नाभिः प्रजानां निहिता जनानाम् ।  
प्रोता त्वमोता विचितिः क्रमाणां प्रजापतिश्छन्दमयो विगर्भः ॥
ekakshara.en: He is the all-knower; the Protector of the world; the firm nave of all born beings. He exists lengthwise and crosswise, the sum total of (things) spread out in order. He is the birthless (of wondrous births) Prajapati sung in the Vedas.

ekakshara.sk:
सामैश्चिदन्तो विरजश्च बाहुं हिरण्मयं वेदविदां वरिष्ठम् ।  
यमध्वरे ब्रह्मविदः स्तुवन्ति सामैर्यजुर्भिः क्रतुभिस्त्वमेव ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou art He whom in the sacrifices the knowers of Brahman adore with Vedic Songs, formulas and rites and the soma juice; as the final (Truth) devoid of all admixture, as the golden, the best of Veda-knowers.

ekakshara.sk:
त्वं स्त्री पुमांस्त्वं च कुमार एकस्त्वं वै कुमारी ह्यथ भूस्त्वमेव ।  
त्वमेव धाता वरुणश्च राजा त्वं वत्सरोऽग्न्यर्यम एव सर्वम् ॥
ekakshara.en: Thou alone art the woman, the man; again Thou art the boy and the girl. Thou art the ordainer, the King Varuna, the Year, Aryama, the all.

ekakshara.sk:
मित्रः सुपर्णश्चन्द्र इन्द्रो वरुणो रुद्रस्त्वष्टा विष्णुः सविता गोपतिस्त्वम् ।  
त्वं विष्णुर्भूतानि तु त्रासि दैत्यांस्त्वयावृतं जगदुद्भवगर्भः ॥
ekakshara.en: (Thou art) Mitra, the bright-feathered (Garuda), the moon, Indra, Varuna, Rudra, Tvastar, Vishnu, Savitar, the lord of light, Thou Vishnu protectest all beings from the demoniac; the world is encompassed by Thee; Thou the womb of all that is born; Thou art the earth, the atmosphere, the firmament; Thou art the self-born; Thou art turned towards all.

ekakshara.sk:
त्वं भूर्भुवः स्वस्त्वं हि स्वयंभूरथ विश्वतोमुखः ।  
य एवं नित्यं वेदयते गुहाशयं प्रभु पुराणं सर्वभूतं हिरण्मयम् ।  
हिरण्मयं बुद्धिमतां परां गतिं स बुद्धिमान्बुद्धिमतीत्य तिष्ठतीत्युपनिषत् ॥
ekakshara.en: Whoso thus knows the eternal Dweller in the cave (of the heart), the ancient Lord who has become the all, the golden, the supreme goal of the wise, is wise. He stays transcending all (forms of) wisdom. This is the secret doctrine.

isavasya.sk:
ईशा वास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्‌ ।  
तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्‌ ॥
isavasya.en:
All this—whatever exists in this changing universe—should be  
covered by the Lord. Protect the Self by renunciation. Lust not  
after any man's wealth.

isavasya.sk:
कुर्वन्नेवेह कर्माणि जिजीविषेत् शतं समाः ।  
एवं त्वयि नान्यथेतोऽस्ति न कर्म लिप्यते नरे ॥
isavasya.en:
If a man wishes to live a hundred years on this earth, he should  
live performing action. For you, who cherish such a desire and  
regard yourself as a man, there is no other way by which you  
can keep work from clinging to you.

isavasya.sk:
असुर्या नाम ते लोका अन्धेन तमसावृताः ।  
तांस्ते प्रेत्याभिगच्छन्ति ये के चात्महनो जनाः ॥
isavasya.en:
Verily, those worlds of the asuras are enveloped in blind  
darkness; and thereto they all repair after death who are slayers  
of Atman.

isavasya.sk:
अनेजदेकं मनसो जवीयो नैनद्देवा आप्नुवन् पूर्वमर्षत्‌ ।  
तद्धावतोऽन्यानत्येति तिष्ठत् तस्मिन्नपो मातरिश्वा दधाति ॥
isavasya.en:
That non—dual Atman, though never stirring, is swifter than  
the mind. The senses cannot reach It, for It moves ever in front.  
Though standing still, It overtakes others who are running.  
Because of Atman, Vayu, the World Soul apportions the  
activities of all.

isavasya.sk:
तदेजति तन्नैजति तद् दूरे तद्वन्तिके ।  
तदन्तरस्य सर्वस्य तदु सर्वस्यास्य बाह्यतः ॥
isavasya.en:
It moves and moves not; It is far and likewise near. It is inside  
all this and It is outside all this.

isavasya.sk:
यस्तु सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मन्येवानुपश्यति ।  
सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं ततो न विजुगुप्सते ॥
isavasya.en:
The wise man beholds all beings in the Self and the Self in all  
beings; for that reason he does not hate anyone.

isavasya.sk:
यस्मिन् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मैवाभूद् विजानतः ।  
तत्र को मोहः कः शोक एकत्वमनुपश्यतः ॥
isavasya.en:
To the seer, all things have verily become the Self: what  
delusion, what sorrow, can there be for him who beholds that  
oneness?

isavasya.sk:
स पर्यगाच्छुक्रमकायमव्रणमस्नाविरं शुद्धमपापविद्धम्‌ ।  
कविर्मनीषी परिभूः स्वयम्भूर्याथातथ्यतोऽर्थान्‌ व्यदधात् शाश्वतीभ्यः समाभ्यः ॥
isavasya.en:
It is He who pervades all—He who is bright and bodiless,  
without scar or sinews, pure and by evil unpierced; who is the  
Seer, omniscient, transcendent and uncreated. He has duly  
allotted to the eternal World—Creators their respective duties.

isavasya.sk:
अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽविद्यामुपासते ।  
ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ विद्यायां रताः ॥
isavasya.en:
Into a blind darkness they enter who are devoted to ignorance  
(rituals); but into a greater darkness they enter who engage in  
knowledge of a deity alone.

isavasya.sk:
अन्यदेवाहुर्विद्ययाऽन्यदाहुरविद्यया ।  
इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे ॥
isavasya.en:
One thing, they say, is obtained from knowledge; another, they  
say, from ignorance. Thus we have heard from the wise who  
have taught us this.

isavasya.sk:
विद्याञ्चाविद्याञ्च यस्तद्वेदोभयं सह ।  
अविद्यया मृत्युं तीर्त्वा विद्ययाऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
isavasya.en:
He who is aware that both knowledge and ignorance should be  
pursued together, overcomes death through ignorance and  
obtains immortality through knowledge.

isavasya.sk:
अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽसम्भूतिमुपासते ।  
ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ सम्भूत्यां रताः ॥
isavasya.en:
Into a blind darkness they enter who worship only the  
unmanifested prakriti; but into a greater darkness they enter  
who worship the manifested Hiranyagarbha.

isavasya.sk:
अन्यदेवाहुः सम्भवादन्यदाहुरसम्भवात्‌ ।  
इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे ॥
isavasya.en:
One thing, they say, is obtained from the worship of the  
manifested; another, they say, from the worship of the  
unmanifested. Thus we have heard from the wise who taught us this.

isavasya.sk:
सम्भूतिञ्च विनाशञ्च यस्तद्वेदोभयं सह ।  
विनाशेन मृत्युं तीर्त्वा सम्भूत्याऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
isavasya.en:
He who knows that both the unmanifested prakriti and the  
manifested Hiranyagarbha should be worshipped together,  
overcomes death by the worship of Hiranyagarbha and obtains  
immortality through devotion to prakriti.

isavasya.sk:
हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सत्यस्यापिहितं मुखम्‌ ।  
तत् त्वं पूषन्नपावृणु सत्यधर्माय दृष्टये ॥
isavasya.en:
The door of the Truth is covered by a golden disc. Open it,  
O Nourisher! Remove it so that I who have been worshipping the  
Truth may behold It.

isavasya.sk:
पूषन्नेकर्षे यम सूर्य प्राजापत्य व्यूह रश्मीन्‌ समूह ।  
तेजो यत् ते रूपं कल्याणतमं तत्ते पश्यामि योऽसावसौ पुरुषः सोऽहमस्मि ॥
isavasya.en:
O Nourisher, lone Traveller of the sky! Controller! O Sun,  
Offspring of Prajapati! Gather Your rays; withdraw Your light.  
I would see, through Your grace, that form of Yours which is  
the fairest. I am indeed He, that Purusha, who dwells there.

isavasya.sk:
वायुरनिलममृतमथेदं भस्मांतं शरीरम्‌ ।  
ॐ क्रतो स्मर कृतं स्मर क्रतो स्मर कृतं स्मर ॥
isavasya.en:
Now may my breath return to the all—pervading, immortal  
Prana! May this body be burnt to ashes! Om. O mind,  
remember, remember all that I have done.

isavasya.sk:
अग्ने नय सुपथा राये अस्मान्‌ विश्वानि देव वयुनानि विद्वान्‌ ।  
युयोध्यस्मज्जुहुराणमेनो भूयिष्ठां ते नम‍उक्तिं विधेम ॥
isavasya.en:
O Fire, lead us by the good path for the enjoyment of the fruit  
of our action. You know, O god, all our deeds. Destroy our sin  
of deceit. We offer, by words, our salutations to you.

katha.sk:
ॐ उशन्‌ ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसं ददौ।  
तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ॥
katha.en: Vajasravasa, desiring, gave all he had. Now Vajasravasa had a son named Nachiketas.

katha.sk: तं ह कुमारं सन्तं दक्षिणासु नीयमानासु श्रद्धाऽऽविवेश। सोऽमन्यत ॥
katha.en: As the gifts were led past, faith took possession of him who was yet a boy unwed and he pondered:

katha.sk:
पीतोदका जग्धतृणा दुग्धदोहा निरिन्द्रियाः।  
अनन्दा नाम ते लोकास्तान्स गच्छति ता ददत्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Cattle that have drunk their water, eaten their grass, yielded their milk, worn out their organs, of undelight are the worlds which he reaches who gives such as these.”

katha.sk:
स होवाच पितरं तत कस्मै मां दास्यसीति।  
द्वितीयं तृतीयं तं होवाच मृत्यवे त्वा ददामीति ॥
katha.en: He said to his father, “Me, O my father, to whom wilt thou give?” A second time and a third he said it, and he replied, “To Death I give thee.”

katha.sk:
बहूनामेमि प्रथमो बहूनामेमि मध्यमः।  
किं स्विद्यमस्य कर्तव्यं यन्मयाद्य करिष्यति ॥
katha.en: “Among many I walk the first, among many I walk the midmost; something Death means to do which today by me he will accomplish.

katha.sk:
अनुपश्य यथा पूर्वे प्रतिपश्य तथाऽपरे।  
सस्यमिव मर्त्यः पच्यते सस्यमिवाजायते पुनः ॥
katha.en: “Look back and see, even as were the men of old,-look round!-even so are they that have come after. Mortal man withers like the fruits of the field and like the fruits of the field he is born again.”

katha.sk:
वैश्वानरः प्रविशत्यतिथिर्ब्राह्मणो गृहान्‌।  
तस्यैतां शान्तिं कुर्वन्ति हर वैवस्वतोदकम्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Fire is the Brahmin who enters as a guest the houses of men; him thus they appease. Bring, O son of Vivasvan, the water of the guest-rite.

katha.sk:
आशाप्रतीक्षे संगतं सूनृतां चेष्टापूर्वे पुत्रपशूंश्च सर्वान्‌।  
एतद्‌ वृङ्क्ते पुरुषस्याल्पमेधसो यस्यानश्नन्वसति ब्राह्मणो गृहे ॥
katha.en: “That man of little understanding in whose house a Brahmin dwells fasting, all his hope and his expectation and all he has gained and the good and truth that he has spoken and the wells he has dug and the sacrifices he has offered and all his sons and his cattle are torn from him by that guest unhonoured.”

katha.sk:
तिस्रो रात्रीर्यदवात्सीर्गृहे मेऽनश्नन्ब्रह्मन्नतिथिर्नमस्यः।  
नमस्तेऽस्तु ब्रह्मन्स्वस्ति मेऽस्तु तस्मात्प्रति त्रीन्वरान्वृणीष्व ॥
katha.en: “Because for three nights thou hast dwelt in my house, O Brahmin, a guest worthy of reverence,-salutation to thee, O Brahmin, on me let there be the weal,-therefore three boons do thou choose; for each night a boon.”

katha.sk:
शान्तसंकल्पः सुमना यथा स्याद्वीतमन्युर्गौतमो माभि मृत्यो।  
त्वत्प्रसृष्टं माभिवदेत्प्रतीत एतत्त्रयाणां प्रथमं वरं वृणे ॥
katha.en: “Tranquillised in his thought and serene of mind be the Gautama, my father, let his passion over me pass away from him; assured in heart let him greet me from thy grasp delivered; this boon I choose, the first of three.”

katha.sk:
यथा पुरस्ताद्‌ भविता प्रतीत औद्दालकिरारुणिर्मत्प्रसृष्टः।  
सुखं रात्रीः शयिता वीतमन्युस्त्वां ददृशिवान्मृत्युमुखात्प्रमुक्तम्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Even as before assured in heart and by me released shall he be, Auddalaki Aruni, thy father; sweetly shall he sleep through the nights and his passion shall pass away from him, having seen thee from death's jaws delivered.”

katha.sk:
स्वर्गे लोके न भयं किञ्चनास्ति न तत्र त्वं न जरया बिभेति।  
उभे तीर्त्वाऽशनायापिपासे शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
katha.en: “In heaven fear is not at all, in heaven, O Death, thou art not, nor old age and its terrors; crossing over hunger and thirst as over two rivers, leaving sorrow behind, the soul in heaven rejoices.

katha.sk:
स त्वमग्निं स्वर्ग्यमध्येषि मृत्यो प्रब्रूहि त्वं श्रद्दधानाय मह्यम्‌।  
स्वर्गलोका अमृतत्वं भजन्त एतद्‌ द्वितीयेन वृणे वरेण ॥
katha.en: “Therefore that heavenly Flame2 which thou, O Death, studiest, expound unto me, for I believe. They who win their world of heaven, have immortality for their portion. This for the second boon I have chosen.”

katha.sk:
प्र ते ब्रवीमि तदु मे निबोध स्वर्ग्यमग्निं नचिकेतः प्रजानन्‌।  
अनन्तलोकाप्तिमथो प्रतिष्ठां विद्धि त्वमेतं निहितं गुहायाम्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Hearken to me and understand, O Nachiketas; I declare to thee that heavenly Flame, for I know it. Know this to be the possession of infinite existence and the foundation and the thing hidden in the secret cave of our being.”

katha.sk:
लोकादिमग्निं तमुवाच तस्मै या इष्टका यावतीर्वा यथा वा।  
स चापि तत्प्रत्यवदद्यथोक्तमथास्य मृत्युः पुनरेवाह तुष्टः ॥
katha.en: Of the Flame that is the world's beginning he told him and what are the bricks and how many and the way of their setting; and Nachiketas too repeated it even as it was told; then Death was pleased and said to him yet farther;

katha.sk:
तमब्रवीत्प्रीयमाणो महात्मा वरं तवेहाद्य ददामि भूयः।  
तवैव नाम्ना भविताऽयमग्निः सृङ्कां चेमामनेकरूपां गृहाण ॥
katha.en: Yea; the Great Soul was gratified and said to him, “Yet a farther boon today I give thee; for even by thy name shall this Fire be called; this necklace also take unto thee, a necklace of many figures.

katha.sk:
त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रिभिरेत्य सन्धिं त्रिकर्मकृत्तरति जन्ममृत्यू।  
ब्रह्मजज्ञं देवमीड्यं विदित्वा निचाय्येमां शान्तिमत्यन्तमेति ॥
katha.en: “Whoso lights the three fires of Nachiketas and comes to union with the Three and does the triple works, beyond birth and death he crosses; for he finds the God of our adoration, the Knower who is born from the Brahman, whom having beheld he attains to surpassing peace.

katha.sk:
त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रयमेतद्विदित्वा य एवं विद्वांश्चिनुते नाचिकेतम्‌।  
स मृत्युपाशान्पुरतः प्रणोद्य शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
katha.en: “When a man has the three flames of Nachiketas and knows this that is Triple, when so knowing he beholds the Flame of Nachiketas, then he thrusts from in front of him the meshes of the snare of death; leaving sorrow behind him he in heaven rejoices.

katha.sk:
एष तेऽग्निर्नचिकेतः स्वर्ग्यो यमवृणीथा द्वितीयेन वरेण।  
एतमग्निं तवैव प्रवक्श्यन्ति जनासस्तृतीयं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व ॥
katha.en: “This is the heavenly Flame, O Nachiketas, which thou hast chosen for the second boon; of this Flame the peoples shall speak that it is thine indeed. A third boon choose, O Nachiketas.”

katha.sk:
येयं प्रेते विचिकित्सा मनुष्येऽस्तीत्येके नायमस्तीति चैके।  
एतद्विद्यामनुशिष्टस्त्वयाऽहं वराणामेष वरस्तृतीयः ॥
katha.en: “This debate that there is over the man who has passed and some say 'This he is not' and some that he is, that, taught by thee, I would know; this is the third boon of the boons of my choosing.”

katha.sk:
देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं पुरा न हि सुविज्ञेयमणुरेष धर्मः।  
अन्यं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व मा मोपरोत्सीरति मा सृजैनम्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Even by the gods was this debated of old; for it is not easy of knowledge, since very subtle is the law of it. Another boon choose, O Nachiketas; importune me not, nor urge me; this, this abandon.”

katha.sk:
देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं किल त्वं च मृत्यो यन्न सुज्ञेयमात्थ।  
वक्ता चास्य त्वादृगन्यो न लभ्यो नान्यो वरस्तुल्य एतस्य कश्चित्‌ ॥
katha.en: “Even by the gods was this debated, it is sure, and thou thyself hast said that it is not easy of knowledge; never shall I find another like thee to tell of it, nor is there any other boon that is its equal.”

katha.sk:
शतायुषः पुत्रपौत्रान्वृणीष्व बहून्पशून्हस्तिहिरण्यमश्वान्‌।  
भूमेर्महदायतनं वृणीष्व स्वयं च जीव शरदो यावदिच्छसि ॥
katha.en: “Choose sons and grandsons who shall live each a hundred years, choose much cattle and elephants and gold and horses; choose a mighty reach of earth and thyself live for as many years as thou listest.

katha.sk:
एतत्तुल्यं यदि मन्यसे वरं वृणीष्व वित्तं चिरजीविकां च।  
महाभूमौ नचिकेतस्त्वमेधि कामानां त्वां कामभाजं करोमि ॥
katha.en: “This boon if thou deemest equal to that of thy asking, choose wealth and long living; possess thou, O Nachiketas, a mighty country; I give thee thy desire of all desirable things for thy portion.

katha.sk:
ये ये कामा दुर्लभा मर्त्यलोके सर्वान्कामांश्छन्दतः प्रार्थयस्व।  
इमा रामाः सरथाः सतूर्या न हीदृशा लम्भनीया मनुष्यैः।  
आभिर्मत्प्रत्ताभिः परिचारयस्व नचिकेतो मरणं माऽनुप्राक्शीः ॥
katha.en: ye ye kämäù martyaloke durlabhäù (tän) sarvän kämän chandataù prärthayasva| imäù sarathäù satüryäù rämäù édåçäù manuñyaiù na hi lambhanéyäù | matprattäbhiù äbhiù paricärayasva| naciketaù maraëam mä anupräkñéù ||

katha.sk:
श्वोभावा मर्त्यस्य यदन्तकैतत्सर्वेन्द्रियाणां जरयन्ति तेजः।  
अपि सर्वं जीवितमल्पमेव तवैव वाहास्तव नृत्यगीते ॥
katha.en: “Until the morrow mortal man has these things, O Ender, and they wear away all this keenness and glory of his senses; nay, all life is even for a little. Thine are these chariots and thine the dancing of these women and their singing.

katha.sk:
न वित्तेन तर्पणीयो मनुष्यो लप्स्यामहे वित्तमद्राक्श्म चेत्त्वा।  
जीविष्यामो यावदीशिष्यसि त्वं वरस्तु मे वरणीयः स एव ॥
katha.en: “Man is not to be satisfied by riches, and riches we shall have if we have beheld thee and shall live as long as thou shalt be lord of us. This boon and no other is for my choosing.

katha.sk:
अजीर्यताममृतानामुपेत्य जीर्यन्मर्त्यः क्वधःस्थः प्रजानन्‌।  
अभिध्यायन्वर्णरतिप्रमोदानतिदीर्घे जीविते को रमेत ॥
katha.en: “Who that is a mortal man and grows old and dwells down upon the unhappy earth, when he has come into the presence of the ageless Immortals and knows, yea, who when he looks very close at beauty and enjoyment and pleasure, can take delight in overlong living?

katha.sk:
यस्मिन्निदं विचिकित्सन्ति मृत्यो यत्साम्पराये महति ब्रूहि नस्तत्‌।  
योऽयं वरो गूढमनुप्रविष्टो नान्यं तस्मान्नचिकेता वृणीते ॥
katha.en:
“This of which they thus debate, O Death, declare to me, even that which is in the great passage; than this boon which enters in into the secret that is hidden from us, no other chooses Nachiketas.”

## Part 2

katha.sk:
अन्यच्छ्रेयोऽन्यदुतैव प्रेयस्ते उभे नानार्थे पुरुषं सिनीतः।  
तयोः श्रेय आददानस्य साधुर्भवति हीयतेऽर्थाद्य उ प्रेयो वृणीते ॥
katha.en: One thing is the good and quite another thing is the pleasant, and both seize upon a man with different meanings. Of these whoso takes the good, it is well with him; he falls from the aim of life who chooses the pleasant.

katha.sk:
श्रेयश्च प्रेयश्च मनुष्यमेतस्तौ सम्परीत्य विविनक्ति धीरः।  
श्रेयो हि धीरोऽभि प्रेयसो वृणीते प्रेयो मन्दो योगक्शेमाद्‌वृणीते ॥
katha.en: çreyaù ca preyaù ca manuñyam etaù| dhéraù tau samparétya vivinakti| dhéraù hi preyasaù çreyaù abhivåëéte| mandaù yogakñemät preyaù våëéte ||

katha.sk:
स त्वं प्रियान्प्रियरूपांश्च कामानभिध्यायन्नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः।  
नैतां सृङ्कां वित्तमयीमवाप्तो यस्यां मज्जन्ति बहवो मनुष्याः ॥
katha.en: And thou, O Nachiketas, hast looked close at the objects of desire, at pleasant things and beautiful, and thou hast cast them from thee; thou hast not entered into the net of riches in which many men sink to perdition.

katha.sk:
दूरमेते विपरीते विषूची अविद्या या च विद्येति ज्ञाता।  
विद्याभीप्सिनं नचिकेतसं मन्ये न त्वा कामा बहवोऽलोलुपन्त ॥
katha.en: For far apart are these, opposite, divergent, the one that is known as the Ignorance and the other the Knowledge. But Nachiketas I deem truly desirous of the knowledge whom so many desirable things could not make to lust after them.

katha.sk:
अविद्यायामन्तरे वर्तमानाः स्वयं धीराः पण्डितम्मन्यमानाः।  
दन्द्रम्यमाणाः परियन्ति मूढा अन्धेनैव नीयमाना यथान्धाः ॥
katha.en: They who dwell in the ignorance, within it, wise in their own wit and deeming themselves very learned, men bewildered are they who wander about round and round circling like blind men led by the blind.

katha.sk:
न साम्परायः प्रतिभाति बालं प्रमाद्यन्तं वित्तमोहेन मूढम्‌।  
अयं लोको नास्ति पर इति मानी पुनः पुनर्वशमापद्यते मे ॥
katha.en: The childish wit bewildered and drunken with the illusion of riches cannot open its eyes to see the passage to heaven; for he that thinks this world is and there is no other, comes again and again into Death's thraldom.

katha.sk:
श्रवणायापि बहुभिर्यो न लभ्यः शृण्वन्तोऽपि बहवो यं न विद्युः।  
आश्चर्यो वक्ता कुशलोऽस्य लब्धाश्चर्यो ज्ञाता कुशलानुशिष्टः ॥
katha.en: He that is not easy even to be heard of by many, and even of those that have heard they are many who have not known Him,-a miracle is the man that can speak of Him wisely or is skilful to win Him, and when one is found, a miracle is the listener who can know God even when taught of Him by the knower.

katha.sk:
न नरेणावरेण प्रोक्त एष सुविज्ञेयो बहुधा चिन्त्यमानः।  
अनन्यप्रोक्ते गतिरत्र नास्त्यणीयान् ह्यतर्क्यमणुप्रमाणात्‌ ॥
katha.en: An inferior man cannot tell you of Him; for thus told thou canst not truly know Him, since He is thought of in many aspects. Yet unless told of Him by another thou canst not find thy way there to Him; for He is subtler than subtlety and that which logic cannot reach.

katha.sk:
नैषा तर्केण मतिरापनेया प्रोक्तान्येनैव सुज्ञानाय प्रेष्ठ।  
यां त्वमापः सत्यधृतिर्बतासि त्वादृङ नो भूयान्नचिकेतः प्रष्टा ॥
katha.en: This wisdom is not to be had by reasoning, O beloved Nachiketas; only when told thee by another it brings real knowledge,-the wisdom which thou hast gotten. Truly thou art steadfast in the Truth! Even such a questioner as thou art may I meet with always.

katha.sk:
जानाम्यहं शेवधिरित्यनित्यं न ह्यध्रुवैः प्राप्यते हि ध्रुवं तत्‌।  
ततो मया नाचिकेतश्चितोऽग्निरनित्यैर्द्रव्यैः प्राप्तवानस्मि नित्यम्‌ ॥
katha.en: I know of treasure that it is not for ever; for not by things unstable shall one attain That which is stable; therefore I heaped the fire of Nachiketas, and by the sacrifice of transitory things I won the Eternal.

katha.sk:
कामस्याप्तिं जगतः प्रतिष्ठां क्रतोरानन्त्यमभयस्य पारम्‌।  
स्तोमं अहदुरुगायं प्रतिष्ठां दृष्ट्वा धृत्या धीरो नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः ॥
katha.en: When thou hast seen in thy grasp, O Nachiketas, the possession of desire and firm foundation of this world and an infinity of power and the other shore of security and praise and scope and wide moving and firm foundation, wise and strong in steadfastness thou didst cast these things from thee.

katha.sk:
तं दुर्दर्शं गूढमनुप्रविष्टं गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम्‌।  
अध्यात्मयोगाधिगमेन देवं मत्वा धीरो हर्षशोकौ जहाति ॥
katha.en: Realising God by attainment to Him through spiritual Yoga, even the Ancient of Days who hath entered deep into that which is hidden and is hard to see, for he is established in our secret being and lodged in the cavern heart of things, the wise and steadfast man casts far from him joy and sorrow.

katha.sk:
एतच्छ्रुत्वा सम्परिगृह्य मर्त्यः प्रवृह्य धर्म्यमणुमेतमाप्य।  
स मोदते मोदनीयं हि लब्ध्वा विवृतं सद्म नचिकेतसं मन्ये ॥
katha.en: When mortal man has heard, when he has grasped, when he has forcefully separated the Righteous One from his body and won that subtle Being, then he has delight, for he has got that which one can indeed delight in. Verily I deem of Nachiketas as a house wide open.

katha.sk:
अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मादन्यत्रास्मात्कृताकृतात्‌।  
अन्यत्र भूताच्च भव्याच्च यत्तत्पश्यसि तद्वद ॥
katha.en: Tell me of That which thou seest otherwhere than in virtue and otherwhere than in unrighteousness, otherwhere than in the created and the uncreated, otherwhere than in that which has been and that which shall be.

katha.sk:
सर्वे वेदा यत्पदमामनन्ति तपांसि सर्वाणि च यद्वदन्ति।  
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति तत्ते पदं संग्रहेण ब्रवीम्योमित्येतत्‌ ॥
katha.en: The seat and goal that all the Vedas glorify and which all austerities declare, for the desire of which men practise holy living, of That will I tell thee in brief compass. OM is that goal, O Nachiketas.

katha.sk:
एतद्‌ध्येवाक्षरं ब्रह्म एतद्‌ध्येवाक्षरं परम्‌।  
एतद्‌ध्येवाक्षरं ज्ञात्वा यो यदिच्छति तस्य तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: For, this Syllable is the Most High: this Syllable if one know, whatsoever one shall desire, it is his.

katha.sk:
एतदालम्बनं श्रेष्ठमेतदालम्बनं परम्‌।  
एतदालम्बनं ज्ञात्वा ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
katha.en: This support is the best, this support is the highest, knowing this support one grows great in the world of the Brahman.

katha.sk:
न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चिन्नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित्‌।  
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
katha.en: That Wise One is not born, neither does he die; he came not from anywhere, neither is he anyone; he is unborn, he is everlasting, he is ancient and sempiternal, he is not slain in the slaying of the body.

katha.sk:
हन्ता चेन्मन्यते हन्तुं हतश्चेन्मन्यते हतम्‌।  
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
katha.en: If the slayer think that he slays, if the slain think that he is slain, both of these have not the knowledge. This slays not, neither is He slain.

katha.sk:
अणोरणीयान्महतो महीयानात्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्‌।  
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ॥
katha.en: Finer than the fine, huger than the huge the Self hides in the secret heart of the creature: when a man strips himself of will and is weaned from sorrow, then he beholds Him, purified from the mental elements he sees the greatness of the Self-being.

katha.sk:
आसीनो दूरं व्रजति शयानो याति सर्वतः।  
कस्तं मदामदं देवं मदन्यो ज्ञातुमर्हति ॥
katha.en: Seated He journeys far off, lying down He goes everywhere. Who other than I is fit to know God, even Him who is rapture and the transcendence of rapture?

katha.sk:
अशरीरं शरीरेष्वनवस्थेष्ववस्थितम्‌।  
महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
katha.en: Realising the Bodiless in bodies, the Established in things unsettled, the Great and Omnipresent Self, the wise and steadfast soul grieves no longer.

katha.sk:
नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन।  
यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यस्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम्‌ ॥
katha.en: The Self is not to be won by eloquent teaching, nor by brain power, nor by much learning: but only he whom this being chooses can win Him, for to him this Self bares His body.

katha.sk:
नाविरतो दुश्चरितान्नाशान्तो नासमाहितः।  
नाशान्तमानसो वापि प्रज्ञानेनैनमाप्नुयात्‌ ॥
katha.en: None who has not ceased from doing evil, or who is not calm, or not concentrated in his being, or whose mind has not been tranquillised, can by wisdom attain to Him.

katha.sk:
यस्य ब्रह्म च क्षत्रं च उभे भवत ओदनः।  
मृत्युर्यस्योपसेचनं क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ॥
katha.en:
He to whom the sages are as meat and heroes as food for His eating and Death is an ingredient of His banquet, how thus shall one know of Him where He abideth?

## Part 3

katha.sk:
ऋतं पिबन्तौ सुकृतस्य लोके गुहां प्रविष्टौ परमे परार्धे।  
छायातपौ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति पञ्चाग्नयो ये च त्रिणाचिकेताः ॥
katha.en: There are two that drink deep of the Truth in the world of work well accomplished: they are lodged in the secret plane of being and in the highest kingdom of the most High is their dwelling: as of light and shade the knowers of the Brahman speak of them and those of the five fires and those who have the three fires of Nachiketas.

katha.sk:
यः सेतुरीजानानामक्षरं ब्रह्म यत्परम्‌।  
अभयं तितीर्षतां पारं नाचिकेतं शकेमहि ॥
katha.en: May we have strength to kindle Agni Nachiketas, for he is the bridge of those who do sacrifice and he is Brahman supreme and imperishable, and the far shore of security to those who would cross this ocean.

katha.sk:
आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि शरीरं रथमेव तु।  
बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च ॥
katha.en: Know the body for a chariot and the soul for the master of the chariot: know Reason for the charioteer and the mind for the reins only.

katha.sk:
इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयांस्तेषु गोचरान्‌।  
आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः ॥
katha.en: The senses they speak of as the steeds and the objects of sense as the paths in which they move; and One yoked with Self and the mind and the senses is the enjoyer, say the thinkers.

katha.sk:
यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्ययुक्तेन मनसा सदा  
तस्येन्द्रियाण्यवश्यानि दुष्टाश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
katha.en: Now he that is without knowledge with his mind ever unapplied, his senses are to him as wild horses and will not obey the driver of the chariot.

katha.sk:
यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति युक्तेन मनसा सदा  
तस्येन्द्रियाणि वश्यानि सदश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
katha.en: But he that has knowledge with his mind ever applied, his senses are to him as noble steeds and they obey the driver.

katha.sk:
यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्यमनस्कः सदाऽशुचिः।  
न स तत्पदमाप्नोति संसारं चाधिगच्छति ॥
katha.en: Yea, he that is without knowledge and is unmindful and is ever unclean, reaches not that goal, but wanders in the cycle of phenomena.

katha.sk:
यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति समनस्कः सदा शुचिः।  
स तु तत्पदमाप्नोति यस्माद् भूयो न जायते ॥
katha.en: But he that has knowledge and is mindful and pure always, reaches that goal whence he is not born again.

katha.sk:
विज्ञानसारथिर्यस्तु मनः प्रग्रहवान्नरः।  
सोऽध्वनः पारमाप्नोति तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम्‌ ॥
katha.en: That man who uses the mind for reins and the knowledge for the driver, reaches the end of his road, the highest seat of Vishnu.

katha.sk:
इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः।  
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः ॥
katha.en: Than the senses the objects of sense are higher; and higher than the objects of sense is the Mind; and higher than the Mind is the faculty of knowledge; and than that is the Great Self higher.

katha.sk:
महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः।  
पुरुषान्न परं किंचित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः ॥
katha.en: And higher than the Great Self is the Unmanifest and higher than the Unmanifest is the Purusha: than the Purusha there is none higher: He is the culmination, He is the highest goal of the journey.

katha.sk:
एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽऽत्मा न प्रकाशते।
दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः ॥
katha.en: The secret Self in all existences does not manifest Himself to the vision: yet is He seen by the seers of the subtle by a subtle and perfect understanding.

katha.sk:
यच्छेद्वाङ्मनसी प्राज्ञस्तद्यच्छेज्ज्ञान आत्मनि।  
ज्ञानमात्मनि महति नियच्छेत्तद्यच्छेच्छान्त आत्मनि ॥
katha.en: Let the wise man restrain speech in his mind and mind in his self of knowledge, and knowledge in the Great Self, and that again let him restrain in the Self that is at peace.

katha.sk:
उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत।  
क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥
katha.en: Arise, awake, find out the great ones and learn of them; for sharp as a razor's edge, hard to traverse, difficult of going is that path, say the sages.

katha.sk:
अशब्दमस्पर्शमरूपमव्ययं तथाऽरसं नित्यमगन्धवच्च यत्‌।  
अनाद्यनन्तं महतः परं ध्रुवं निचाय्य तन्मृत्युमुखात्‌ प्रमुच्यते ॥
katha.en: That in which sound is not, nor touch, nor shape, nor diminution, nor taste, nor smell, that which is eternal, and It is without end or beginning, higher than the Great Self and stable,-that having seen, from the mouth of death there is deliverance.

katha.sk:
नाचिकेतमुपाख्यानं मृत्युप्रोक्तं सनातनम्‌।  
उक्त्वा श्रुत्वा च मेधावी ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
katha.en: The man of intelligence having spoken or heard the eternal story of Nachiketas wherein Death was the speaker, grows great in the world of the Brahman.

katha.sk:
य इमं परमं गुह्यं श्रावयेद्‌ ब्रह्मसंसदि।  
प्रयतः श्राद्धकाले वा तदानन्त्याय कल्पते।  
तदानन्त्याय कल्पत इति ॥
katha.en:
He who being pure recites this supreme secret at the time of the Shraddha in the assembly of the Brahmins, that turns for him to infinite existence.

# Chapter 2

## Part 1

katha.sk:
पराञ्चिखानि व्यतृणत्स्वयंभूस्तस्मात्पराङ्पश्यति नान्तरात्मन्‌।  
कश्चिद्धीरः प्रत्यगात्मानमैषदावृत्तचक्षुरमृतत्वमिच्छन्‌ ॥
katha.en: The Self-born hath set the doors of the body to face outward, therefore the soul of a man gazeth outward and not at the Self within; hardly a wise man here and there desiring immortality turneth his eyes inward and seeth the Self within him.

katha.sk:
पराचः कामाननुयन्ति बालास्ते मृत्योर्यन्ति विततस्य पाशम्‌।  
अथ धीरा अमृतत्वं विदित्वा ध्रुवमध्रुवेष्विह न प्रार्थयन्ते ॥
katha.en: The rest childishly follow after desire and pleasure and walk into the snare of Death who gapeth wide for them. But calm souls having learned of immortality seek not for permanence in the things of this world that pass and are not.

katha.sk:
येन रूपं रसं गन्धं शब्दान्स्पर्शांश्च मैथुनान्‌।  
एतेनैव विजानाति किमत्र परिशिष्यते। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: By the Self one knoweth taste and form and smell, by the Self one knoweth sound and touch and the joy of man with woman; what is there left in this world of which the Self not knoweth? This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
स्वप्नान्तं जागरितान्तं चोभौ येनानुपश्यति।  
महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
katha.en: The calm soul having comprehended the great Lord, the omnipresent Self by whom one beholdeth both to the end of dream and to the end of waking, ceaseth from grieving.

katha.sk:
य इमं मध्वदं वेद आत्मानं जीवमन्तिकात्‌।  
ईशानं भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: He that hath known from very close this Eater of sweetness, the Jiva, the Self within that is lord of what was and what shall be, shrinketh not thereafter from aught nor abhorreth any. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
यः पूर्वं तपसो जातमद्‌भ्यः पूर्वमजायत।  
गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तं यो भूतेभिर्व्यपश्यत। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: He is the seer that seeth Him who came into being before austerity and was before the waters; deep in the heart of the creature he seeth Him, for there He standeth by the mingling of the elements. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
या प्राणेन संभवत्यदितिर्देवतामयी।  
गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तीं या भूतेभिर्व्यजायत। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: This is Aditi, the mother of the Gods, who was born through the Prana and by the mingling of the elements had her being; deep in the heart of things she has entered, there she is seated. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
अरण्योर्निहितो जातवेदा गर्भ इव सुभृतो गर्भिणीभिः।  
दिवे दिवे ईड्यो जागृवद्भिर्हविष्मद्भिर्मनुष्येभिरग्निः। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: As a woman carrieth with care the unborn child in her womb, so is the Master of knowledge lodged in the tinders, and day by day should men worship him who live their waking life and stand before him with sacrifice; for he is that Agni. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
यतश्चोदेति सूर्योऽस्तं यत्र च गच्छति।  
तं देवाः सर्वेऽर्पितास्तदु नात्येति कश्चन। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: He from whom the sun riseth and to whom the sun returneth, and in Him are all the Gods established,-none passeth beyond Him. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
यदेवेह तदमुत्र यदमुत्र तदन्विह।  
मृत्योः स मृत्युमाप्नोति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥
katha.en: What is in this world is also in the other, and what is in the other, that again is in this; who thinketh he sees difference here, from death to death he goeth.

katha.sk:
मनसैवेदमाप्तव्यं नेह नानाऽस्ति किंचन।  
मृत्योः स मृत्युं गच्छति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥
katha.en: Through the mind must we understand that there is nothing in this world that is really various; who thinketh he sees difference here, from death to death he goeth.

katha.sk:
अंगुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो मध्य आत्मनि तिष्ठति।  
ईशानो भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: The Purusha who is seated in the midst of ourself is no larger than the finger of a man. He is the lord of what was and what shall be; Him having seen one shrinketh not from aught nor abhorreth any. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
अंगुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो ज्योतिरिवाधूमकः।  
ईशानो भूतभव्यस्य स एवाद्य स उ श्वः। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: The Purusha that is within is no larger than the finger of a man; He is like a blazing fire that is without smoke, He is lord of His past and His future. He alone is today and He alone shall be tomorrow. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
यथोदकं दुर्गं वृष्टं पर्वतेषु विधावति।  
एवं धर्मान्पृथक्‌ पश्यंस्तानेवानुविधावति ॥
katha.en: As water that raineth in the rough and difficult places, runneth to many sides on the mountaintops, so he that seeth separate law and action of the one Spirit, followeth in the track of what he seeth.

katha.sk:
यथोदकं शुद्धे शुद्धमासिक्तं तादृगेव भवति।  
एवं मुनेर्विजानत आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥
katha.en:
But as pure water that is poured into pure water, even as it was such it remaineth, so is it with the soul of the thinker who knoweth God, O seed of Gotama.

## Part 2

katha.sk:
पुरमेकादशद्वारमजस्यावक्रचेतसः।  
अनुष्ठाय न शोचति विमुक्तश्च विमुच्यते। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: The Unborn who is not devious-minded hath a city with eleven gates; when He taketh up his abode in it, He grieveth not, but when He is set free from it, that is His deliverance. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
हंसः शुचिषद्वसुरान्तरिक्षसद्धोता वेदिषदतिथिर्दुरोणसत्‌।  
नृषद्वरसदृतसद्व्योमसदब्जा गोजा ऋतजा अद्रिजा ऋतं बृहत्‌ ॥
katha.en: Lo, the Swan whose dwelling is in the purity, He is the Vasu in the interregions, the Sacrificer at the altar, the Guest in the vessel of the drinking; He is in man and in the Great Ones and His home is in the Law and His dwelling is in the firmament; He is all that is born of water and all that is born of earth and all that is born of the mountains. He is the Truth and He is the Mighty One.

katha.sk:
ऊर्ध्वं प्राणमुन्नयत्यपानं प्रत्यगस्यति।  
मध्ये वामनमासीनं विश्वे देवा उपासते ॥
katha.en: This is He that draweth the main breath upward and casteth the lower breath downward. The Dwarf that sitteth in the centre, to Him all the Gods do homage.

katha.sk:
अस्य विस्रंसमानस्य शरीरस्थस्य देहिनः।  
देहाद्विमुच्यमानस्य किमत्र परिशिष्यते। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: When this encased spirit that is in the body falleth away from it, when He is freed from its casing, what is there then that remaineth? This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
न प्राणेन नापानेन मर्त्यो जीवति कश्चन।  
इतरेण तु जीवन्ति यस्मिन्नेतावुपाश्रितौ ॥
katha.en: Man that is mortal liveth not by the breath, no, nor by the lower breath; but by something else we live in which both these have their being.

katha.sk:
हन्त त इदं प्रवक्ष्यामि गुह्यं ब्रह्म सनातनम्‌।  
यथा च मरणं प्राप्य आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥
katha.en: Surely, O Gautama, I will tell thee of this secret and eternal Brahman and likewise what becometh of the soul when one dieth.

katha.sk:
योनिमन्ये प्रपद्यन्ते शरीरत्वाय देहिनः।  
स्थाणुमन्येऽनुसंयन्ति यथाकर्म यथाश्रुतम्‌ ॥
katha.en: For some enter a womb to the embodying of the Spirit and others follow after the Immovable; according to their deeds is their goal and after the measure of their revealed knowledge.

katha.sk:
य एष सुप्तेषु जागर्ति कामं कामं पुरुषो निर्मिमाणः।  
तदेव शुक्रं तद् ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते।  
तस्मिंल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: This that waketh in the sleepers creating desire upon desire, this Purusha, Him they call the Bright One, Him Brahman, Him Immortality, and in Him are all the worlds established; none goeth beyond Him. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
अग्निर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव।  
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
katha.en: Even as one Fire hath entered into the world but it shapeth itself to the forms it meeteth, so there is one Spirit within all creatures but it shapeth itself to form and form; it is likewise outside these.

katha.sk:
वायुर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव।  
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
katha.en: Even as one Air hath entered into the world but it shapeth itself to the forms it meeteth, so there is one Spirit within all creatures but it shapeth itself to form and form; it is likewise outside these.

katha.sk:
सूर्यो यथा सर्वलोकस्य चक्षुर्न लिप्यते चाक्षुषैर्बह्यिदोषैः।  
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा न लिप्यते लोकदुःखेन बाह्यः ॥
katha.en: Even as the Sun is the eye of all this world, yet it is not soiled by the outward blemishes of the visual, so there is one Spirit within all creatures, but the sorrow of this world soils it not, for it is beyond grief and his danger.

katha.sk:
एको वशी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा एकं रूपं बहुधा यः करोति।  
तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीरास्तेषां सुखं शाश्वतं नेतरेषाम्‌ ॥
katha.en: One calm and controlling Spirit within all creatures that maketh one form into many fashions; the calm and strong who see Him in the self as in a mirror, theirs is eternal felicity and 'tis not for others.

katha.sk:
नित्योऽनित्यानां चेतनश्चेतनानामेको बहूनां यो विदधाति कामान्‌।  
तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीरास्तेषां शान्तिः शाश्वती नेतरेषाम्‌ ॥
katha.en: The One Eternal in many transient, the One Conscious in many conscious beings, who being One ordereth the desires of many; the calm and strong who behold Him in the self as in a mirror, theirs is eternal peace and 'tis not for others.

katha.sk:
तदेतदिति मन्यन्तेऽनिर्देश्यं परमं सुखम्‌।  
कथं नु तद्विजानीयां किमु भाति विभाति वा ॥
katha.en: “This is He,” is all they can realise of Him, a highest felicity which none can point to nor any define it. How shall I know of Him whether He shineth or reflecteth one light and another?

katha.sk:
न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः।  
तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ॥
katha.en:
There the Sun cannot shine and the moon has no lustre; all the stars are blind; there our lightnings flash not, neither any earthly fire. For all that is bright is but the shadow of His brightness and by His shining all this shineth.

## Part 3

katha.sk:
ऊर्ध्वमूलोऽवाक्‍शाख एषोऽश्वत्थः सनातनः।  
तदेव शुक्रं तद् ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते।  
तस्मिंल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन। एतद्वै तत्‌ ॥
katha.en: This is the eternal uswattha tree whose roots are aloft, but its branches are downward. It is He that is called the Bright One and Brahman and Immortality, and in Him are all the worlds established; none goeth beyond Him. This is the thing thou seekest.

katha.sk:
यदिदं किं च जगत्सर्वं प्राण एजति निःसृतम्‌।  
महद् भयं वज्रमुद्यतं य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
katha.en: All this universe of motion moveth in the Prana and from the Prana also it proceeded; a mighty terror is He, yea, a thunderbolt uplifted. Who know Him are the immortals.

katha.sk:
भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः।  
भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ॥
katha.en: For fear of Him the fire burneth, for fear of Him the sun giveth heat, for fear of Him Indra and Vayu and Death hasten in their courses.

katha.sk:
इह चेदशकद्‌बोद्धुं प्राक् शरीरस्य विस्रसः।  
ततः सर्गेषु लोकेषु शरीरत्वाय कल्पते ॥
katha.en: If in this world of men and before thy body fall from thee, thou art able to apprehend it, then thou availest for embodiment in the worlds that are His creations.

katha.sk:
यथाऽऽदर्शे तथाऽऽत्मनि यथा स्वप्ने तथा पितृलोके।  
यथाऽप्सु परीव ददृशे तथा गन्धर्वलोके छायातपयोरिव ब्रह्मलोके ॥
katha.en: In the self one seeth God as in a mirror but as in a dream in the world of the fathers, and as in water one seeth the surface of an object, so one seeth Him in the world of the Gandharvas; but He is seen as light and shade in the heaven of the Spirit.

katha.sk:
इन्द्रियाणां पृथग्भावमुदयास्तमयौ च यत्‌।  
पृथगुत्पद्यमानानां मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
katha.en: The calm soul having comprehended the separateness of the senses and the rising of them and their setting and their separate emergence putteth from him pain and sorrow.

katha.sk:
इन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनो मनसः सत्त्वमुत्तमम्‌।  
सत्त्वादधि महानात्मा महतोऽव्यक्तमुत्तमम्‌ ॥
katha.en: The mind is higher than the senses, and above the mind is the thought, and above the thought is the mighty Spirit, and above the Mighty One is the Unmanifest.

katha.sk:
अव्यक्तात्तु परः पुरुषो व्यापकोऽलिङ्ग एव च।  
यं ज्ञात्वा मुच्यते जन्तुरमृतत्वं च गच्छति ॥
katha.en: But highest above the Unmanifest is the Purusha who pervadeth all and alone hath no sign nor feature. Mortal man knowing Him is released into immortality.

katha.sk:
न संदृशे तिष्ठति रूपमस्य न चक्षुषा पश्यति कश्चनैनम्‌।  
हृदा मनीषा मनसाऽभिक्लृप्तो य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
katha.en: He hath not set His body within the ken of seeing, neither doth any man with the eye behold Him, but to the heart and mind and the supermind He is manifest. Who know Him are the immortals.

katha.sk:
यदा पञ्चावतिष्ठन्ते ज्ञानानि मनसा सह।  
बुद्धिश्च न विचेष्टते तामाहुः परमां गतिम्‌ ॥
katha.en: When the five senses cease and are at rest and the mind resteth with them and the Thought ceaseth from its workings, that is the highest state, say thinkers.

katha.sk:
तां योगमिति मन्यन्ते स्थिरामिन्द्रियधारणाम्‌।  
अप्रमत्तस्तदा भवति योगो हि प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥
katha.en: The state unperturbed when the senses are imprisoned in the mind, of this they say “it is Yoga”. Then man becomes very vigilant, for Yoga is the birth of things and their ending.

katha.sk:
नैव वाचा न मनसा प्राप्तुं शक्यो न चक्षुषा।  
अस्तीति ब्रुवतोऽन्यत्र कथं तदुपलभ्यते ॥
katha.en: Not with the mind hath man the power to see God, no, nor by speech nor with the eye. Unless one saith “He is,” how can one become sensible of Him?

katha.sk:
अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धव्यस्तत्त्वभावेन चोभयोः।  
अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धस्य तत्त्वभावः प्रसीदति ॥
katha.en: One must apprehend Him in the concept “He is” and also in His essential principle, but when he hath grasped Him as the Is, then the essential of Him dawneth upon a man.

katha.sk:
यदा सर्वे प्रमुच्यन्ते कामा येऽस्य हृदि श्रिताः।  
अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्यत्र ब्रह्म समश्नुते ॥
katha.en: When every desire that harboureth in the heart of a man hath been loosened from its moorings, then this mortal putteth on immortality; even here he enjoyeth Brahman in this human body.

katha.sk:
यथा सर्वे प्रभिद्यन्ते हृदयस्येह ग्रन्थयः।  
अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्येतावद्ध्यनुशासनम्‌ ॥
katha.en: When all the strings of the heart are rent asunder, even here in this human birth, then the mortal becometh immortal. This is the whole teaching of the Scriptures.

katha.sk:
शतं चैका च हृदयस्य नाड्यस्तासां मूर्धानमभिनिःसृतैका।  
तयोर्ध्वमायन्नमृतत्वमेति विश्वङ्ङन्या उत्क्रमणे भवन्ति ॥
katha.en: A hundred and one are the nerves of the heart and of all these only one issueth out through the head of a man; by this the soul mounteth up to its immortal home but the rest lead him to all sorts and conditions of births in his passing.

katha.sk:
अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषोऽन्तरात्मा सदा जनानां हृदये संनिविष्टः।  
तं स्वाच्छरीरात्प्रवृहेन्मुञ्जादिवेषीकां धैर्येण।  
तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतं तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतमिति ॥
katha.en: The Purusha, the Spirit within, who is no larger than the finger of a man is seated for ever in the heart of creatures; one must separate Him with patience from one's own body as one separates from a blade of grass its main fibre. Thou shalt know Him for the Bright Immortal, yea, for the Bright Immortal.

katha.sk:
मृत्युप्रोक्तां नचिकेतोऽथ लब्ध्वा विद्यामेतां योगविधिं च कृत्स्नम्‌।  
ब्रह्मप्राप्तो विरजोऽभूद्विमृत्यु रन्योऽप्येवं यो विदध्यात्ममेव ॥
katha.en: Thus did Nachiketas with Death for his teacher win the God-knowledge; he learned likewise the whole ordinance of the Yoga: thereafter he obtained Brahman and became void of stain and void of death. So shall another be who cometh likewise to the science of the Spirit.

kena.sk:
केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः।  
केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः।  
केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति।  
चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति ॥
kena.en: By whom missioned falls the mind shot to its mark? By whom yoked moves the first life - breath forward on its paths? By whom impelled is this word that men speak? What god set eye and ear to their workings?

kena.sk:
श्रोत्रस्य श्रोत्रं मनसो मनो यत्।  
वाचो ह वाचं स उ प्राणस्य प्राणः।  
चक्षुषश्चक्षुरतिमुच्य धीराः।  
प्रेत्यास्माल्लोकादमृता भवन्ति ॥
kena.en: That which is hearing of our hearing, mind of our mind, speech of our speech, that too is life of our life -breath and sight of our sight. The wise are released beyond and they pass from this world and become immortal.

kena.sk:
न तत्र चक्षुर्गच्छति न वाग्गच्छति नो मनो  
न विद्मो न विजानीमो यथैतदनुशिष्यात्।  
अन्यदेव तद्विदितादथो अविदितादधि।  
इति शुश्रुम पूर्वेषां ये नस्तद्व्याचचक्षिरे ॥
kena.en: There sight travels not, nor speech, nor the mind. We know It not nor can distinguish how one should teach of It: for It is other than the known; It is there above the unknown. It is so we have heard from men of old who declared That to our understanding.

kena.sk:
यद्वाचानभ्युदितं येन वागभ्युद्यते।  
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते ॥
kena.en: That which is unexpressed by the word, that by which the word is expressed, know That to be the Brahman and not this which men follow after here.

kena.sk:
यन्मनसा न मनुते येनाहुर्मनो मतम्‌।  
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते ॥
kena.en: That which thinks not by the mind, that by which the mind is thought, know That to be the Brahman and not this which men follow after here.

kena.sk:
यच्चक्षुषा न पश्यति येन चक्षूंषि पश्यति।  
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते ॥
kena.en: That which sees not with the eye, that by which one sees the eye's seeings, know That to be the Brahman and not this which men follow after here.

kena.sk:
यच्छ्रोत्रेण न शृणोति येन श्रोत्रमिदं श्रुतम्‌।  
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते ॥
kena.en: That which hears not with the ear, that by which the ear's hearing is heard, know That to be the Brahman and not this which men follow after here.

kena.sk:
यत्प्राणेन न प्राणिति येन प्राणः प्रणीयते।  
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते ॥
kena.en:
That which breathes not with the breath, that by which the life -breath is led forward in its paths, know That to be the Brahman and not this which men follow after here.

## Part 2

kena.sk:
यदि मन्यसे सुवेदेति दभ्रमेवापि नूनं त्वं वेत्थ ब्रह्मणो रूपम्‌।  
यदस्य त्वं यदस्य देवेष्वथ नु मीमांस्यमेव ते मन्ये विदितम्‌ ॥
kena.en: If thou thinkest that thou knowest It well, little indeed dost thou know the form of the Brahman. That of It which is thou, that of It which is in the gods, this thou hast to think out. I think It known.

kena.sk:
नाहं मन्ये सुवेदेति नो न वेदेति वेद च।  
यो नस्तद्वेद तद्वेद नो न वेदेति वेद च ॥
kena.en: I think not that I know It well and yet I know that It is not unknown to me. He of us who knows It, knows That; he knows that It is not unknown to him.

kena.sk:
यस्यामतं तस्य मतं मतं यस्य न वेद सः।  
अविज्ञातं विजानतां विज्ञातमविजानताम्‌ ॥
kena.en: He by whom It is not thought out, has the thought of It; he by whom It is thought out, knows It not. It is unknown to the discernment of those who discern of It, by those who seek not to discern of It, It is discerned.

kena.sk:
प्रतिबोधविदितं मतममृतत्वं हि विन्दते।  
आत्मना विन्दते वीर्यं विद्यया विन्दतेऽमृतम्‌ ॥
kena.en: When It is known by perception that reflects It, then one has the thought of It, for one finds immortality; by the self one finds the force to attain and by the knowledge one finds immortality.

kena.sk:
इह चेदवेदीदथ सत्यमस्ति।  
न चेदिहावेदीन्महती विनष्टिः।  
भूतेषु भूतेषु विचित्य धीराः।  
प्रेत्यास्माल्लोकादमृता भवन्ति ॥
kena.en:
If here one comes to that knowledge, then one truly is; if here one comes not to the knowledge, then great is the perdition. The wise distinguish That in all kinds of becomings and they pass forward from this world and become immortal.

## Part 3

kena.sk:
ब्रह्म ह देवेभ्यो विजिग्ये तस्य ह ब्रह्मणो विजये देवा अमहीयन्त।  
त ऐक्शन्तास्माकमेवायं विजयोऽस्माकमेवायं महिमेति ॥
kena.en: The Eternal conquered for the gods and in the victory of the Eternal the gods grew to greatness. They saw, “Ours the victory, ours the greatness.”

kena.sk: तद्धैषां विजज्ञौ तेभ्यो ह प्रादुर्बभूव तन्न व्यजानत किमिदं यक्षमिति ॥
kena.en: The Eternal knew their thought and appeared before them; and they knew not what was this mighty Daemon.

kena.sk: तेऽग्निमब्रुवन् जातवेद एतद्विजानीहि किमेतद्यक्षमिति तथेति ॥
kena.en: They said to Agni, “O thou that knowest all things born, learn of this thing, what may be this mighty Daemon,” and he said, “So be it.”

kena.sk: तदभ्यद्रवत्तमभ्यवदत्कोऽसीत्यग्निर्वा अहमस्मीत्यब्रवीज्जातवेदा वा अहमस्मीति ॥
kena.en: He rushed towards the Eternal and It said to him, “Who art thou?” “I am Agni,” he said, “I am he that knows all things born.”

kena.sk: तस्मिंस्त्वयि किं वीर्यमित्यपीदं सर्वं दहेयं यदिदं पृथिव्यामिति ॥
kena.en: “Since such thou art, what is the force in thee?” “Even all this I could burn, all that is upon the earth.”

kena.sk: तस्मै तृणं निदधावेतद्दहेति तदुपप्रेयाय सर्वजवेन तन्न शशाक दग्धुं स तत एव निववृते नैतदशकं विज्ञातुं यदेतद्यक्शमिति ॥
kena.en: The Eternal set before him a blade of grass; “This burn;” and he made towards it with all his speed, but could not burn it. There he ceased, and turned back; “I could not know of It, what might be this mighty Daemon.”

kena.sk: अथ वायुमब्रुवन् वायवेतद्विजानीहि किमेतद्यक्शमिति तथेति ॥
kena.en: Then they said to Vayu, “O Vayu, this discern, what is this mighty Daemon.” He said, “So be it.”

kena.sk: तदभ्यद्रवत्तमभ्यवदत् कोऽसीति वायुर्वा अहमस्मीत्यब्रवीन्मातरिश्वा वा अहमस्मीति ॥
kena.en: He rushed upon That; It said to him, “Who art thou?” “I am Vayu,” he said, “and I am he that expands in the Mother of things.”

kena.sk: तस्मिंस्त्वयि किं वीर्यमित्यपीदं सर्वमाददीय यदिदं पृथिव्यामिति ॥
kena.en: “Since such thou art, what is the force in thee?” “Even all this I can take for myself, all this that is upon the earth.”

kena.sk: तस्मै तृणं निदधावेतदादत्स्वेति तदुपप्रेयाय सर्वजवेन तन्न शशाकादातुं स तत एव निववृते नैतदशकं विज्ञातुं यदेतद्यक्शमिति ॥
kena.en: That set before him a blade of grass; “This take.” He went towards it with all his speed and he could not take it. Even there he ceased, even thence he returned; “I could not discern of That, what is this mighty Daemon.”

kena.sk: अथेन्द्रमब्रुवन् मघवन्नेतद्विजानीहि किमेतद्यक्शमिति तथेति तदभ्यद्रवत् तस्मात्तिरोदधे ॥
kena.en: Then they said to Indra, “Master of plenitudes, get thou the knowledge, what is this mighty Daemon.” He said, “So be it.” He rushed upon That. That vanished from before him.

kena.sk: स तस्मिन्नेवाकाशे स्त्रियमाजगाम बहुशोभमानामुमां हैमवतीं तां होवाच किमेतद्यक्शमिति ॥
kena.en:
He in the same ether came upon the Woman, even upon Her who shines out in many forms, Uma daughter of the snowy summits. To her he said, “What was this mighty Daemon?”

## Part 4

kena.sk: सा ब्रह्मेति होवाच ब्रह्मणो वा एतद्विजये महीयध्वमिति ततो हैव विदाञ्चकार ब्रह्मेति ॥
kena.en: She said to him, “It is the Eternal. Of the Eternal is this victory in which ye shall grow to greatness.” Then alone he came to know that this was the Brahman.

kena.sk: तस्माद्वा एते देवा अतितरामिवान्यान्देवान्यदग्निर्वायुरिन्द्रस्ते ह्येनन्नेदिष्ठं पस्पर्शुस्ते ह्येनत्प्रथमो विदाञ्चकार ब्रह्मेति ॥
kena.en: Therefore are these gods as it were beyond all the other gods, even Agni and Vayu and Indra, because they came nearest to the touch of That...

kena.sk: तस्माद् वा इन्द्रोऽतितरामिवान्यान्देवान्स ह्येनन्नेदिष्ठं पस्पर्श स ह्येनत्प्रथमो विदाञ्चकार ब्रह्मेति ॥
kena.en: Therefore is Indra as it were beyond all the other gods because he came nearest to the touch of That, because he first knew that it was the Brahman.

kena.sk: तस्यैष आदेशो यदेतद्विद्युतो व्यद्युतदा इतीन्न्यमीमिषदा इत्यधिदैवतम्‌ ॥
kena.en: Now this is the indication of That,-as is this flash of the lightning upon us or as is this falling of the eyelid, so in that which is of the gods.

kena.sk: अथाध्यात्मं यद्देतद् गच्छतीव च मनोऽनेन चैतदुपस्मरत्यभीक्श्णं सङ्कल्पः ॥
kena.en: Then in that which is of the Self,-as the motion of this mind seems to attain to That and by it afterwards the will in the thought continually remembers It.

kena.sk: तद्ध तद्वनं नाम तद्वनमित्युपासितव्यं स य एतदेवं वेदाभि हैनम् सर्वाणि भूतानि संवाञ्छन्ति॥
kena.en: The name of That is “That Delight”; as That Delight one should follow after It. He who so knows That, towards him verily all existences yearn.

kena.sk: उपनिषदं भो ब्रूहीत्युक्ता त उपनिषद् ब्राह्मीं वाव त उपनिषदमब्रूमेति ॥
kena.en: Thou hast said “Speak to me Upanishad”; spoken to thee is Upanishad. Of the Eternal verily is the Upanishad that we have spoken.

kena.sk: तस्यै तपो दमः कर्मेति प्रतिष्ठा वेदाः सर्वाङ्गानि सत्यमायतनम्‌ ॥
kena.en: Of this knowledge austerity and self-conquest and works are the foundation, the Vedas are all its limbs, truth is its dwelling place.

kena.sk: यो वा एतामेवं वेदापहत्य पाप्मानमनन्ते स्वर्गे लोके ज्येये प्रतितिष्ठति प्रतितिष्ठति ॥
kena.en: He who knows this knowledge, smites evil away from him and in that vaster world and infinite heaven finds his foundation, yea, he finds his foundation.
